taskSummary: Apply the repository landing page masthead treatment to the ResearchOps home page, remove the home breadcrumb, add a high-fidelity ResearchOps SVG illustration, and fix the failing unit test assertion caused by route-scoped body classes.

read: GOV.UK Design System home page image evidence
DWP Design System home page image evidence

decisions: Reused the repository page's route-scoped masthead treatment, but removed the home breadcrumb because it is not needed on the home page.
Kept the home body marker researchops-home-front-page in the GOV.UK renderer context.
Kept the inverse header, service navigation and phase-banner styling route-scoped to the home page.
Added a decorative SVG to the home masthead, hidden from assistive technology with empty alt text and role=presentation.

Built the SVG around recognisable research operations artefacts: study plan card, participant operations panel, moderated session panel, live notes card, evidence trail and ResearchOps service dashboard.
Used grouped SVG structure, GOV.UK palette, connected dotted workflow paths and detailed UI artefacts rather than a low-fidelity icon.
Added explicit image dimensions in Nunjucks and rendered HTML to constrain the illustration in the one-third masthead column.
Removed the added home masthead route-state test after the failure investigation showed the actual failing contract was the shared GOV.UK integration test's exact body-class assertion.
Updated tests/govuk-frontend-integration-route-state.test.js to assert govuk-template__body as a class token so route-scoped body marker classes are allowed.

validation.result: identified tests/govuk-frontend-integration-route-state.test.js exact body-class assertion as the unit test failure
passed on Node 20 and Node 22 after the assertion update

validation.command: Accessibility audit, Render GOV.UK pages, Lychee, BDD and Validate ResearchOps

validation.result: no review threads were present at the time of checking

residualRisks: The home masthead should be checked visually in browser at desktop and mobile widths.

The raw external GOV.UK and DWP SVG files could not be fetched by the web tool, so the detailed implementation was benchmarked directly against the repository SVG code and the official pages' image usage evidence.
